hypothesis: If adaptive, multi-representation diversity constraints informed by NaviDiv are applied during REINVENT4 reinforcement learning for singlet fission molecule generation, then the generated libraries will exhibit significantly higher chemical diversity (lower mean pairwise similarity, more clusters, greater fragment/scaffold uniqueness) without reducing the number of high Chemprop-scoring candidates relative to unconstrained baselines, because dynamic penalties suppress overrepresented patterns while preserving property optimization.
